dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9714 IH: Capping Appointed Positions Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scholten (for herself, Mr. Walkinshaw, and Ms. Norton) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To limit the total number of schedule C and schedule G positions in the Federal Government, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Capping Appointed Positions Act.

section.header: Limitation on schedule C and schedule G positions in Federal Government
section.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, in fiscal year 2027 and each fiscal year thereafter, the total number of positions in the executive branch of the Government of a confidential or policy-determining character under schedule C of subpart C of part 213 of title 5, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation), and in schedule G in the excepted service (established by Executive Order 14317 relating to Creating Schedule G in the Excepted Service) may not exceed 1,600.
